message: 🌱 Add confidential-containers: [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers mission (#651)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "confidential-containers-171-rfc-initdata-specification-for-confidential-containe",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "confidential-containers: [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers",
+    "description": "[RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current confidential-containers deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your confidential-containers version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n confidential-containers -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers\nhelm list -n confidential-containers 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working confidential-containers installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review confidential-containers configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant confidential-containers configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n confidential-containers -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers\nkubectl get configmap -n confidential-containers -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=confidential-containers\n```\nCurrently, we are facing some typical problems in CoCo.\n\n1. How to configure KBS certificates every launch of TEE without embed certs inside guest images? This is not restricted to only KBS certs."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers",
+        "description": "> Enlightening guest components to query the OPA engine seems a bit clunky to me. That will be more complex and slower (performance probably not significant) than just reading a config file. \n\nThis is in the same category as enlightening the guest components to support attestation or fetching\n```yaml\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Pod\nmetadata:\nname: pod1\nannotations:\n  io.katacontainers.config.runtime.cc_init_data: \n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n confidential-containers -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers\nkubectl get events -n confidential-containers --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"[RFC] Initdata Specification for Confidential Containers\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> Enlightening guest components to query the OPA engine seems a bit clunky to me. That will be more complex and slower (performance probably not significant) than just reading a config file. \n\nThis is in the same category as enlightening the guest components to support attestation or fetching things from KBS.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "apiVersion: v1\nkind: Pod\nmetadata:\nname: pod1\nannotations:\n  io.katacontainers.config.runtime.cc_init_data: ",
+        "{\n  \"algorithm\": \"sha384\",\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"data\": {\n    \"attestation-agent.json\": \"ewogICJhYV9rYmNfcGFyYW1zIjogImNjX2tiYzo6aHR0cDovLzEyNy4wLjAuMTo4MDgwIgp9\"\n  }\n  \"digest\": \"b15d418e8d2821635f38bcd01dafc5c6d50c7fa2bc1450f69f1278f258db1360983916d42accd122c9c1e8c785a3e7fb\"\n}",
+        "{\n    \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n    \"algorithm\": \"<the-hash-algorithm-to-calculate-digest-field>\"\n    \"data\": \"<JSON-object>\",\n    \"digest\": \"<digest-of-data-in-hex>\"\n}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {

+    },

+  },
